skill.name: repo-metadata
skill.description: Generate Backstage-style catalog-info.yaml metadata by analyzing repository structure, documentation, dependencies, integrations, and service patterns.
skill.summary: Create service catalog metadata for repository architecture documentation.
skill.author: AstraBit-CPT

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-severity weak-cryptography and command-execution patterns, but review found they are false positives in YAML examples, schema documentation, and Markdown code fences. The executable Python script performs intended local repository scanning and metadata generation without subprocess execution or outbound network requests. No prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Recursive Local Repository Scanning
low_findings.description: The generator accepts a repository path and recursively reads matching source and metadata files to infer service catalog fields. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should run it only on repositories they are allowed to inspect because local file contents influence the generated output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed code clearly reads local repository files, but it does not transmit them or execute them. The risk is limited to local data exposure through generated metadata.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography hits occur in catalog metadata examples and schema reference text. They describe component fields such as APIs, events, schemas, and services, not cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive hashing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation or YAML field examples. No cryptographic operation, hash validation, cipher selection, or credential handling is present at those locations.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick matches are Markdown inline-code and fenced-code formatting that describe commands or schema fields. The Python script imports subprocess but does not call subprocess, and no reviewed file executes shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found formatting backticks and an unused subprocess import, but no command invocation. The executable path prints generated YAML or JSON only.

low_findings.title: Static Network Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The network pattern is a regular expression used to detect HTTP client libraries in the target repository. It is not an HTTP request made by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code defines search patterns for axios, fetch, RestTemplate, and WebClient. No import or call to a network client library was found in the generator.

content.value_statement: Repository ownership and integration details are often scattered across code and documents. This skill analyzes local repository structure and creates catalog-info.yaml metadata for service catalogs and architecture reviews.
actual_capabilities: Generates catalog-info.yaml content using Backstage-style component metadata.
Infers runtime and framework from package files and dependency patterns.
Classifies repositories as services, gateways, workers, libraries, frontends, or databases.
Detects local integration signals such as HTTP clients, Kafka usage, gRPC clients, and database libraries.
Reads README, INTEGRATIONS, and ARCHITECTURE documents to improve generated metadata.
Provides example catalog files for gateways, workers, libraries, and full templates.
limitations: Domain and owner values may remain unknown without user input.
Detection depends on recognizable file names, dependencies, and code patterns.
Generated routes, topics, and dependency names can require manual review.
The included YAML writer is simple and may not preserve custom formatting.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Service for Catalog Onboarding
use_cases.description: Generate initial catalog metadata so a repository can appear in an internal service catalog.

use_cases.title: Document Microservice Dependencies
use_cases.description: Summarize runtime, framework, APIs, events, and upstream dependencies in a standard file.

use_cases.title: Review Architecture Coverage
use_cases.description: Compare generated metadata with existing architecture documentation before cross-repository analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Metadata
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate catalog-info.yaml metadata for this repository and show the inferred fields that need my review.

prompt_templates.title: Classify Repository Type
prompt_templates.scenario: Check service type before writing metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository and explain whether it should be classified as a service, gateway, worker, library, frontend, or database.

prompt_templates.title: Review Integrations
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate catalog metadata and focus on dependsOn, providesApis, consumesApis, eventProducers, and eventConsumers. Mark uncertain values clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Update Catalog and Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate catalog-info.yaml, compare it with README, INTEGRATIONS, and ARCHITECTURE, then suggest documentation updates for consistency.

output_examples.input: A NestJS API gateway repository with downstream user and order services.
output_examples.output: A reviewed metadata summary that identifies the repository as a gateway, lists platform ownership fields, and marks downstream routes for confirmation.

output_examples.input: A Python worker repository with Kafka consumers and no HTTP routes.
output_examples.output: A catalog metadata draft that classifies the component as a worker and highlights event consumer details that need exact topic names.

output_examples.input: A shared TypeScript utility package with README documentation.
output_examples.output: A library metadata draft with SDK-style API information and a short checklist for confirming ownership and lifecycle.

best_practices: Review owner, domain, lifecycle, routes, and event topics before committing generated metadata.
Run the generator from the intended repository root so paths and names resolve correctly.
Keep README, INTEGRATIONS, and ARCHITECTURE documents current to improve metadata quality.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated metadata with unknown owners or domains in production catalogs.
Do not treat inferred dependencies as authoritative without code owner review.
Do not run repository scanning on directories that contain unrelated sensitive files.

faq.question: What file does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or drafts catalog-info.yaml for repository service catalog metadata.

faq.question: Does it use the Backstage catalog format?
faq.answer: It follows Backstage-style conventions with Astrabit-specific fields for architecture documentation.

faq.question: Can it detect the repository runtime?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks common package files such as package.json, pyproject.toml, go.mod, and build files.

faq.question: Can it identify service dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: It can infer likely dependencies from local integration patterns, but owners should verify every dependency.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence of outbound network requests was found in the reviewed generator script.

faq.question: What should users review manually?
faq.answer: Users should verify owner, domain, lifecycle, route mappings, event topics, API definitions, and dependency names.
